title: Kohayakawa–Kreuter Conjecture (Conjecture 1.3)
statement_text: Let $H_{1},\dots,H_{r}$ be graphs, and suppose that $m_{2}(H_{1})\geq\dotsb\geq m_{2}(H_{r})$ and $m_{2}(H_{2})>1$. There exist constants $C>c>0$ such that $G_{n,p}$ is a.a.s. Ramsey for $(H_{1},\dots,H_{r})$ if $p\geq Cn^{-1/m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})}$, and a.a.s. not Ramsey if $p\leq cn^{-1/m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})}$, where $$m_{2}(H_{1},H_{2})\coloneqq\max\left\{\frac{{\mathsf{e}}(J)}{{\mathsf{v}}(J)-2+1/m_{2}(H_{2})}:J\subseteq H_{1},{\mathsf{v}}(J)\geq 2\right\}.$$
context_text: Kohayakawa and Kreuter formulated this conjecture nearly thirty years ago for the threshold of the asymmetric Ramsey property of $G_{n,p}$ for an arbitrary $r$-tuple of graphs. The assumption $m_{2}(H_{2})>1$ (i.e., $H_2$ is not a forest) was added by Kohayakawa, Schacht, and Spöhel to rule out sporadic counterexamples. Earlier work of Mousset–Nenadov–Samotij, Bowtell–Hancock–Hyde, and Kuperwasser–Samotij–Wigderson had reduced the probabilistic problem to a deterministic graph decomposition conjecture.
paper_contribution: Resolves the deterministic graph decomposition problem to which the conjecture had been reduced, thereby proving the Kohayakawa–Kreuter conjecture in full generality for all $r$-tuples of graphs.
notes: Statement text partially reconstructed from context; the input truncates after 'There exist constants $C>c>0$ such that'. The mixed 2-density formula $m_2(H_1,H_2)$ is taken verbatim from the paper's display math.

title: Conjecture 7.1 (Kuperwasser–Samotij–Wigderson)
statement_text: Let $m>0$ be a real number. Every graph $G$ with $m(G)\leq m$ contains a forest $F\subseteq G$ such that $m_{2}(G-F)\leq m$.
context_text: This conjecture of Kuperwasser, Samotij, and Wigderson strengthens the conclusion of Theorem 1.6 by requiring $F$ to be a forest rather than a pseudoforest, and is in fact a common strengthening of both Theorem 1.6 and Theorem 1.7. If true, it would imply a natural generalization of the Kohayakawa–Kreuter conjecture to the setting where one wishes to avoid a finite family of graphs $\{H_i^{(1)},\dots,H_i^{(t)}\}$ in each color class of $G_{n,p}$.
paper_contribution: The paper proves related graph decomposition results (Theorems 1.6 and 1.7) that are implied by this conjecture, but does not resolve Conjecture 7.1 itself; it is highlighted as a natural open direction.
notes: Original source is Kuperwasser–Samotij–Wigderson [19, Conjecture 1.5], arXiv:2307.16611. Attributed year not determinable from the bibliography entry (listed as preprint).
